_meta.description: Motesart Number System Source — core axioms for the SOM music education model

axioms.name: Sound Before Symbol
axioms.description: Students experience and feel musical concepts through sound before any notation or theory is introduced
axioms.implication: Feel Mode gates require audio interaction before visual/symbolic assessment

axioms.name: Pattern Recognition First
axioms.description: The brain naturally seeks patterns — half/whole step patterns precede scale names
axioms.implication: PHASE_1 and PHASE_2 build pattern vocabulary before PHASE_4 key signatures

axioms.name: Relative Before Absolute
axioms.description: Intervals and relationships between notes matter more than note names initially
axioms.implication: T_HALF_STEP and T_WHOLE_STEP precede T_C_MAJOR_SCALE in the sequence

axioms.name: Mastery Over Coverage
axioms.description: Deep ownership of fewer concepts beats shallow exposure to many
axioms.implication: 5 pilot concepts get full perception modeling before expanding

axioms.name: Evidence-Based Progression
axioms.description: Students advance only when perception data confirms genuine understanding
axioms.implication: Feel Mode gates A/B/C must be passed with required confidence thresholds

axioms.name: Teacher Visibility
axioms.description: Teachers must see real-time evidence of student concept health to intervene effectively
axioms.implication: evidence_summary and concept health dashboards exist for every tracked concept
